phrases.intent_en: Ask for a good spot to picnic along the canal
phrases.text_local: Quel est le meilleur endroit pour pique-niquer le long du canal ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel eh luh meh-YUR ahn-DRWAH poor peek-nee-KAY luh lohn doo kah-NAL
phrases.cultural_note: The Canal Saint-Martin is lined with plane trees and iron footbridges, making it one of Paris's most romantic spots for a picnic. The quays between République and Jaurès are popular on warm evenings, with young Parisians sitting on the stone embankments with wine, cheese, and baguettes.

responses.english: Set up along the Quai de Valmy, it is very pleasant.
responses.phonetic: ahn-stah-LAY voo luh lohn doo kay duh val-MEE, seh treh ah-gray-AH-bluh
responses.french: Installez-vous le long du quai de Valmy, c'est très agréable.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to find vintage and second-hand shops
phrases.text_local: Où peut-on trouver des boutiques vintage et de seconde main dans le quartier ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo puh-TOHN troo-VAY day boo-TEEK veen-TEZH ay duh suh-KOHND mehn dahn luh kar-TYAY
phrases.cultural_note: The Canal Saint-Martin area (10th arrondissement) is the heart of Paris's bobo (bourgeois-bohemian) culture, with numerous independent vintage shops, concept stores, and bookshops on Rue de Marseille, Rue Beaurepaire, and Rue Yves-Toudic.

responses.english: There are second-hand shops on Rue Beaurepaire.
responses.phonetic: eel ee ah day free-puh-REE soor lah roo boh-ruh-PAIR
responses.french: Il y a des friperies sur la Rue Beaurepaire.

phrases.intent_en: Ask how to watch a boat pass through the lock
phrases.text_local: Peut-on regarder les bateaux passer par les écluses ?
phrases.pronunciation: puh-TOHN ruh-gar-DAY lay bah-TOH pah-SAY par layz ay-KLOOZ
phrases.cultural_note: The Canal Saint-Martin has nine locks (écluses) and two swing bridges (ponts tournants) that are operated manually. Watching a barge slowly rise or descend through a lock is a classic Paris experience. The canal was commissioned by Napoleon in 1802 to supply fresh water to Paris.

responses.english: Stay along the quay, the locks operate regularly.
responses.phonetic: res-TAY luh lohn doo kay, layz ay-KLOOZ fonk-SYOHN ray-goo-lyehr-MAHN
responses.french: Restez le long du quai, les écluses fonctionnent régulièrement.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a coffee at a local independent café
phrases.text_local: Un café, s'il vous plaît — vous avez du café de spécialité ?
phrases.pronunciation: uhn kah-FAY, seel voo PLEH — voo zah-VAY doo kah-FAY duh spay-syah-lee-TAY
phrases.cultural_note: The Canal Saint-Martin neighborhood has the highest concentration of specialty coffee (café de spécialité) shops in Paris. Ten Belles (at 10 Rue de la Grange aux Belles) is one of Paris's pioneering specialty coffee roasters and was instrumental in changing Parisian coffee culture.

responses.english: Try the café on the Quai de Jemmapes.
responses.phonetic: eh-say-YAY luh kah-FAY soor luh kay duh zheh-MAP
responses.french: Essayez le café sur le quai de Jemmapes.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the canal goes from here
phrases.text_local: Où va le canal depuis ici ? Il rejoint la Seine ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo vah luh kah-NAL duh-PWEE ee-SEE ? eel ruh-ZHWAHN lah sehn
phrases.cultural_note: The Canal Saint-Martin runs from the Bassin de la Villette (north) underground through the 10th arrondissement, emerging near Place de la République and continuing south to the Seine at the Port de l'Arsenal near Bastille. The underground section was covered in the 1860s to create the Boulevard Richard-Lenoir above.

responses.english: The canal goes to the Bassin de la Villette, to the north.
responses.phonetic: luh kah-NAL vah zhoos-KOH bah-SAN duh lah vee-LET, oh nor
responses.french: Le canal va jusqu'au bassin de la Villette, au nord.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a restaurant recommendation along the canal
phrases.text_local: Pouvez-vous me conseiller un bon restaurant au bord du canal ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-VAY voo muh kohn-seh-YAY uhn bohn reh-stoh-RAHN oh bor doo kah-NAL
phrases.cultural_note: The canal is flanked by excellent independent restaurants. Chez Prune (on Quai de Valmy) is an iconic canal-side café-restaurant. The area has a strong East Asian food scene as well, with Japanese, Korean, and Vietnamese restaurants on the surrounding streets.

responses.english: There are good restaurants along the quay, a bit further up.
responses.phonetic: eel ee ah duh bohn res-toh-RAHN luh lohn doo kay, uhn puh ploo oh
responses.french: Il y a de bons restaurants le long du quai, un peu plus haut.
